name: truncated structured header — one byte
description: A structured-mode payload whose columns demand more bits than available. Byte 0x0a is the first byte of enc({a:1}): it encodes mode=structured, len=1, vflags=mode-1 (all-refs), but the subsequent vrefs/krefs/keys/vtypes/nums columns require additional bytes that are absent. The decoder reads past the zero-extended buffer tail and throws.

name: truncated four-key object — one byte
description: First byte of enc({a:1,b:2,c:3,d:4}) — structured mode, len=4, all columns truncated. The column data required to decode 4 entries far exceeds the 1-byte buffer; the decoder reads past the end.

name: truncated four-key object — two bytes
description: First two bytes of enc({a:1,b:2,c:3,d:4}). The vflags/vrefs columns demand more bits than the 2-byte buffer provides.
